tldr: Replace Braze's proprietary engagement suite with Novu's open-source, self-hostable notification infrastructure.
why_switch: Open-source core — option to self-host and avoid vendor lock-in
Generous free tier vs. Braze paid-only model
Developer-first SDKs for in-app feed, email, push, SMS
Lower lock-in score (templates and workflows live in your codebase via Novu's framework)
export_method: Pull users, attributes, and events through Braze's REST API; for templates and Canvas flows, export from the dashboard where available or document them manually. If your Braze plan includes Currents, configure a one-time historical dump to S3 for richer event history. Confirm push subscription state is included so you don't lose opt-ins.
import_method: Stand up a Novu workspace (cloud) or self-host. Add subscribers via Novu's API, configure providers (FCM/APNs/SES/Twilio/etc.) under Integrations, and define workflows using Novu's framework — workflows live as code, which gives you version control. Replace Braze SDK calls with Novu's trigger calls in your application. For in-app feed, embed Novu's prebuilt component or call the API. Always verify endpoints against current Novu docs.

concept_mapping.note: subscriberId is the equivalent of external_id.
Workflows are code-defined — re-author rather than auto-import.
Novu is event-driven; scheduled blasts need an external scheduler or Novu's scheduling features — check docs.
Verify current segmentation capabilities in Novu docs.
UI component differs; design review needed.

gotchas: Self-hosting Novu adds ops burden — only choose it if you have infra capacity.
Workflows-as-code is a paradigm shift from Canvas — train the team.
Provider credentials (APNs, FCM, ESP) must be re-uploaded to Novu.
Personalization syntax differs; don't copy Liquid templates verbatim.
Plan a dual-run period to validate delivery rates before sunsetting Braze.
Some advanced Braze features (e.g., Predictive Suite) have no direct equivalent.

email.subject: Migrating from Braze to Novu
email.body:
Hi team,

Proposing we move our notification stack from Braze to Novu. Novu is open-source and gives us workflow-as-code, lower cost, and the option to self-host if we want full control.

Plan: export subscribers, attributes, push tokens, templates, and journeys from Braze; recreate workflows in Novu's framework; swap Braze SDK calls for Novu triggers; run both in parallel for a lifecycle window.

Estimated effort: 4-8 weeks. Main risks: workflow paradigm shift, no 1:1 mapping for advanced Braze features (Predictive Suite, etc.), and self-host ops cost if we go that route.

Happy to walk through the migration checklist.
